title: eriogonum ochrocephalum var. agnellum
intro: Eriogonum ochrocephalum var. agnellum, commonly known as Sulphur Flower Buckwheat or Sulphur Buckwheat, is a rare, flowering plant native to California in the United States. This shrubby perennial plant belongs to the Polygonaceae family and is characterized by its bright yellow flowers, which bloom from July to September. Traditionally, Sulphur Flower Buckwheat has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to support the health benefits of Sulphur Flower Buckwheat, including its ability to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system.
